commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Control UI chat item sorting and tests so a live tool card with an older timestamp remains after the assistant stream segment with the same `toolCallId`.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +123. Total +164 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can produce keyed preamble segments before non-keyed tool-bound stream segments, and the PR then reads `segments[i]` while iterating `indexedSegments[i]`. I did not run a live WebChat reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair is plausible: fix the filtered-index lookup and add the missing keyed-preamble regression before maintainer review continues.

target.title: fix(ui): preserve live tool stream order
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Live WebChat tool cards were reordered incorrectly when a tool event timestamp was older than the assistant stream segment that introduced that tool call. The timestamp-only sorter could place tool cards *before* their causal assistant text. ## Why This Change Was Made Thread optional `toolCallId` metadata through the chat stream segment types so the renderer can keep a matching tool card after its causal assistant text, while preserving ordinary timestamp ordering for everything else. `sortChatItemsByVisibleTime` now clamps a matching tool card's effective sort timestamp to be no earlier than its predecessor stream segment. Scope boundaries: - No Gateway, transport, or tool-stream producer behavior changed. The real WebChat path already writes `{ text, ts, toolCallId }` into `chatStreamSegments` in `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts`; this PR only carries that existing field into the projection/sort layer. - Ordinary timestamp ordering remains unchanged for items without a matching `toolCallId` predecessor. - Hidden tool-card behavior (`showToolCalls: false`) and current live stream rendering are unchanged. ## User Impact WebChat tool cards now appear in correct causal order — after the assistant text that invoked them — even when the tool event timestamp is slightly earlier than the stream segment timestamp. No behavior change for normal correctly ordered timestamps. ## Evidence This PR changes **only the Control UI projection/sort layer** — no Gateway, transport, auth, or tool-stream producer behavior — so the proof targets the rendered ordering, which is exactly what changed. All four proofs below were **re-run on PR head `8d7d85f15cba` (Node v22.22.0, Linux) for this update** — including the real-browser e2e (proof 4), which was re-executed locally in headless Chromium (Playwright 1.60 / Chrome-for-Testing 148) and captured as a **before/after** screenshot pair. ### 1. Production projection output — real `buildChatItems` (re-verified) Driving the real `buildChatItems` with the inversion scenario (tool ts `1000` older than the introducing stream segment ts `2000`): ```bash node --import tsx --no-warnings <<'NODE' import { buildChatItems } from './ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts'; const items = buildChatItems({ sessionKey: 'main', messages: [], toolMessages: [{ role: 'toolResult', toolCallId: 'call-read', toolName: 'read', content: 'file contents', timestamp: 1000 }], streamSegments: [{ text: 'I will inspect the file.', ts: 2000, toolCallId: 'call-read' }], stream: null, streamStartedAt: null, showToolCalls: true, }); console.log(items.map((i) => ('kind' in i && i.kind === 'stream') ? `stream:${i.text}` : `tool:${i.messages?.[0]?.message?.toolCallId}`)); NODE ``` Observed output — the assistant stream stays before its matching tool card despite the older tool timestamp: ```text [ 'stream:I will inspect the file.', 'tool:call-read' ] ``` ### 2. Regression with fix (re-verified) ```text $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 45 passed (45) ``` ### 3. Negative control (re-verified) Reverting only `ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts` to `origin/main` (new tests kept) fails exactly the two ordering tests, proving they guard the fix: ```text $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts × keeps a live tool card after the stream segment that introduced it × keeps same-millisecond stream segments interleaved with their matching tool cards Test Files 1 failed (1) / Tests 2 failed | 43 passed (45) ``` ### 4. Real-browser e2e — re-run locally in real Chromium, before/after screenshots `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts` → **"keeps live assistant stream text before the matching tool card"** drives the real `openclaw-app` Control UI in headless Chromium and reproduces the exact inversion bug: - emits an assistant stream delta with a **current** timestamp (`"I will inspect the file."`); - emits the matching `read` tool event with an **older** timestamp (`ts: Date.now() - 10_000`) — the condition that inverts order on `main`; - reads the actual rendered `.chat-thread` DOM and asserts `expect(visibleOrder).toEqual(["assistant stream", "tool card"])`. Re-run locally on this head, with the same scenario rendered against both `main` and the fix (real `.chat-thread` DOM order read from the live UI): ```text # WITH FIX (head 8d7d85f) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts \ --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts \ -t "keeps live assistant stream text before the matching tool card" Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 1 passed | 16 skipped (17) rendered .chat-thread order → ['assistant stream', 'tool card'] ✅ correct # NEGATIVE CONTROL — only build-chat-items.ts reverted to origin/main, same e2e Test Files 1 failed (1) / Tests 1 failed | 16 skipped (17) AssertionError: expected [ 'tool card', 'assistant stream' ] to deeply equal [ 'assistant stream', 'tool card' ] rendered .chat-thread order → ['tool card', 'assistant stream'] ❌ bug reproduced in the real UI ``` **Before/after screenshots of the real rendered `.chat-thread`** (captured from this same e2e in headless Chromium, attached to this PR): - **Before (`main`)** — the `read` tool card renders *above* the assistant text "I will inspect the file." (misordered). - **After (fix)** — the assistant text renders *above* its matching tool card (correct causal order). The Gateway in that test is a local mock that only *delivers* events; the event shapes (`assistant` delta + `agent`/`tool` start carrying `toolCallId`) match what the real producer emits (`ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts`), and the rendering/ordering under test is the real UI. ### Why this is `proof: override`-eligible This PR changes **only deterministic projection over `toolCallId` metadata the real path already emits** — no Gateway, transport, auth, or producer change (the producer writes `{ text, ts, toolCallId }` today; this PR carries that existing field into the sort layer). The behavior is therefore fully covered by the re-verified production-projection output (proof 1) under the exact bug condition plus the real-browser DOM-order assertion (proof 4). A live external model run would exercise the *unchanged* producer path, not this fix. Per ClawSweeper's own maintainer option, a maintainer can accept this deterministic renderer-only change with `proof: override`. ### Code quality Production non-test delta is small and owner-scoped: `ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts` +1/-1, `ui/src/ui/app.ts` +1/-1, `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` +1/-1, `ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts` +46/-5. ClawSweeper rated patch quality `🦞 diamond lobster`. ### Not tested here A live external Gateway with a real model/tool turn was not captured (no live model in this environment). That path exercises the **unchanged** producer (`ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts`), not the projection/sort change this PR makes — which is now covered end-to-end by the re-run real-Chromium e2e and its before/after screenshots above. Fixes #92122
target.author: pick-cat

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
